title: Alseodaphne elongata
taxonomy: Alseodaphne elongata is classified within the family Lauraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as laurel or avocet. This family is a part of the order Laurales, which is a group of flowering plants that are often evergreen and have aromatic foliage. The Laurales order is part of the larger clade called magnoliids, which is a group of flowering plants that are thought to be among the most ancient. The magnoliids clade is a part of the larger group called angiosperms, which is a group of flowering plants that are thought to have evolved from a common ancestor. Alseodaphne elongata is native to tropical Africa, and its classification within this broader group reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

In some regions, its leaves and bark are used to treat various health issues. The plant's extracts are also being studied for their antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Alseodaphne elongata is considered a valuable resource for its medicinal properties, but more research is required to fully understand its potential uses.
